pi_items.source: Web search verified 10-50 as standard police code for 'Traffic Accident'. Confirmed in example export file: create_dispatch_call_export.json shows type='10-50 | Vehicle Accident'. Verifier score breakdown in evidence_docs/README.md line 83 confirms exact match.

Web search confirms Vinewood Boulevard is a fictional street in Grand Theft Auto V (GTA V), located in Los Santos. Found at https://gta.fandom.com/wiki/Vinewood_Boulevard_(HD_Universe). Example export file (create_dispatch_call_export.json, line 9) shows street1='Vinewood Boulevard '. Verifier score breakdown in README.md line 82 confirms exact match.

Web search confirms Alta Street is a fictional street in Grand Theft Auto V (GTA V), located in Los Santos. Found at https://gta.fandom.com/wiki/Alta_Street. Example export file (create_dispatch_call_export.json, line 10) shows street2='Alta Street '. Verifier score breakdown in README.md line 83 confirms exact match.

Example export file (create_dispatch_call_export.json, line 11) shows narrative contains: 'THREE-VEHICLE COLLISION', 'INJURIES REPORTED', 'EMS BACKUP REQUESTED'. All four keywords verified present. Verifier score breakdown in README.md line 84 shows '4/4 matched' resulting in 20/20 points.

privileged_info_summary: The OpenCAD dispatch task uses synthetic data from Grand Theft Auto V roleplay environments. The expected call type is 10-50 (Traffic Accident), a valid police dispatch code. The expected location is Vinewood Boulevard and Alta Street intersection, both fictional GTA V streets in Los Santos. The task narrative must contain four keywords: vehicle, accident, injuries, and EMS. All metadata claims have been verified against the example task export file and are consistent with the documented OpenCAD roleplay system architecture.
